Title: RubyLLM
Description: A single, beautiful Ruby framework for all major AI providers. Easily build chatbots, AI agents, RAG applications, content generators, and every AI workflow you can think of.

Title: OpenAI unveils its first custom chip, built by Broadcom | TechCrunch
Description: Named Jalapeño, the new processor was designed specifically for the unique needs of OpenAI's inference systems.

Title: We’re making Bunny DNS free
Description: Your DNS bill shouldn't scale with your traffic. So we removed query fees from Bunny DNS entirely. No per-request billing and no query limits.

Title: Framework's 10G Ethernet module exposes USB-C's complexity
Description:
I've been following WisdPi's development of various 5 Gbps and 10 Gbps Ethernet adapters for the past couple years.
They use newer Realtek Ethernet chips, which sometimes have performance quirks—most frequently encountered under Linux.
In today's video, I tested the new WisdPi 10G Ethernet Expansion Card for Framework computers. It fits in any available Framework Expansion slot—even on the Framework Desktop.
But Expansion Cards use USB-C for their connection to the mainboard—and therein lies the rub...

Title: Founding a Company in Germany: €9,600, 152 Days, and I Still Can’t Send an Invoice
Description: I started a company in Germany in late January. By late June I had spent 9,600 euros, registered two companies, and still cannot issue a single invoice of my...

Title: Vulnerability Reports Are Not Special Anymore
Description: We needed the insight and confidentiality to protect our users, but now that anyone can get the same results from LLM?

Title: In memory of the man who put red and green squiggles under words - The Old New Thing
Description: Starting in Word and expanding to nearly every other word processor, and even things that aren't word processors.
